key_departures.impact: Led the Cowboys in sacks in 2025 with 8.5 despite signing mid-September. Was their most consistent pass rusher after the Parsons trade. Departure leaves a significant void — Dallas did not re-sign him, reportedly due to scheme shift under new DC Christian Parker. Still a free agent as of this writing.

Had just 3 sacks in 17 games with the Cowboys after 10.5 with Washington in 2024 — a clear regression. Door appeared closed on his return per ESPN's Todd Archer. Minor loss given underperformance, but still reduces EDGE depth.

Was a starter but posted a 52.4 PFF grade in 2025. Departure was expected and Cowboys quickly moved to replace the position with Jalen Thompson and P.J. Locke. Creates a need that is largely being addressed.
Departed prior to 2025 season for Seattle, listed as compensatory departure. 11-year Cowboy. Was already gone entering 2025 but noted for comp pick context.

Traded for 2026 third-round pick (No. 92 overall). Had been a 5-year starter with 17 career sacks. Cowboys moved him out despite a 4yr/$80M deal signed last offseason — became expendable after adding Kenny Clark and Quinnen Williams. Cap savings of ~$4.75M in 2026. Receiving a 3rd-round pick is solid return. IDL depth reduced but Kenny Clark and Quinnen Williams remain.
Traded to Titans in a seventh-round pick swap. Pure depth loss — minimal impact on roster.
Season ended early with hamstring injury. Was a disappointment in Dallas; hired because of ties to former DC Eberflus. Cowboys flagged as needing LB upgrade.
Fell out of rotation after Ryan Flournoy surpassed him. With Lamb, Pickens, and Flournoy all expected back, Tolbert seeks opportunity elsewhere. Minimal depth loss at a non-needy position.

contract.notes: Reworked deal post-trade; 2026 cap hit reduced to $5.44M via $13.2M signing bonus and 4 void years. Original 4yr/$96M deal restructured. Source: ESPN/Todd Archer, OTC.

signings.analysis: Pro Bowl edge rusher (2024) with 46.5 career sacks and 7.5 sacks in 2025 — though production dried up in the second half of last season. The trade cost only a 2027 Day 3 pick and the reworked contract is cap-friendly at $5.44M in 2026. Gary gives the Cowboys their best edge rusher but his inconsistency and age (28 with an ACL history) means he is a genuine starter, not an elite solution. EDGE remains a draft need — Donovan Ezeiruaku is also out for all OTAs/minicamp with hip surgery. Edge rushes from the top of the draft still make sense.

contract.notes: Max value $36M. $9.7M signing bonus. Guaranteed salaries and per-game roster bonuses in 2026 and 2027. Source: Spotrac, OTC, ESPN/Todd Archer.

signings.analysis: Three-year deal with $22M guaranteed — a real investment that signals a genuine starter. Thompson, 27, has started 87 of 99 games in his career and brings positional versatility for new DC Christian Parker's scheme. Paired with restructured Malik Hooker, Dallas now has a legitimate starting tandem. Reunites with Cowboys secondary coach Ryan Smith from Arizona. Safety drops from a critical tier-1 need to a low tier-3 depth consideration. A first-round safety pick is now off the table per beat analysts.

contract.notes: $6M signing bonus. 2026/2027 base salaries fully guaranteed. 2 void years for cap purposes. 2026 cap hit ~$3.95M. Source: OTC, ESPN/Adam Schefter.

signings.analysis: Locked up the position before the market exploded. Williams ran for 1,201 yards and 11 TDs in 2025, his first year with Dallas. Two years of real guaranteed money signals genuine commitment. RB was not a top need but locking Williams in at $8M/year — below where the RB market moved in FA — is a major win. RB is a settled, non-draft position for Dallas.

contract.notes: Non-exclusive franchise tag. Long-term deal possible before July 15 deadline. Source: NFL.com, CBS Sports.

signings.analysis: Pickens is secured for 2026 but long-term status is unresolved. His 2025 season (93 rec, 1,429 yds, 9 TDs, Pro Bowl) was elite. The non-exclusive franchise tag allows him to negotiate with other teams, and trade rumors have not fully subsided. WR remains partially flagged until a long-term extension is signed or tag period resolves — if Pickens is traded, WR becomes a critical need. For now: partially addressed with significant uncertainty.

contract.notes: Max value $3M. Source: ESPN/Todd Archer, Blogging the Boys.

signings.analysis: Low-cost retention of the 2022 second-round pick still rebuilding after a torn ACL. Had just 1 sack in 2025 returning from injury. One-year deal with minimal guarantees signals a depth/prove-it role, not a starter solution. Does not move the needle on EDGE as a need — they need more. ESPN noted this signing doesn't take them out of the EDGE market in free agency or the draft.

contract.notes: One-year deal reported at $5M. Source: Blogging the Boys / CBS Sports tracker.

signings.analysis: One-year, depth deal. Locke started 26 games in Denver and provides special teams value. Reunites with DC Christian Parker. With Thompson and Hooker as the starting pair and Markquese Bell also on the roster, Locke is the fourth safety. Does not change the need picture — this is a depth signing behind the primary need-filling move (Thompson).

contract.notes: 1-year, max $3M deal. Source: Blogging the Boys, Dallas Cowboys official site.

signings.analysis: True nose tackle signing to serve a role in Parker's 3-4 scheme. IDL was not a major need after retaining Kenny Clark and Quinnen Williams — and Dallas just traded Odighizuwa and Thomas away. Ogbonnia fills depth at a position that, while a bit thinner, is still anchored by two quality starters. One-year deal at minimum-adjacent money. Low-impact signing.

contract.notes: One-year deal. Exact terms not publicly reported; CBS Sports tracker lists 1 year. Source: Dallas Cowboys official site, CBS Sports.

signings.analysis: Fourth quarterback behind Prescott, Joe Milton III, and Will Grier. Career record of 5-13. This is a camp/developmental arm with no impact on the QB need picture. Dak Prescott leads the offense; QB is not a need.

contract.notes: Re-signing reported but contract terms not yet disclosed. Source: Blogging the Boys, Yahoo Sports.

signings.analysis: Young developmental edge rusher brought back on presumably a minimum or near-minimum deal. Does not address the starting EDGE need. Pure depth/developmental retention.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Jalen Thompson signed to a 3yr/$33M deal with $22M guaranteed — a genuine starter investment. He reunites with secondary coach Ryan Smith, starts alongside a restructured Malik Hooker, and gives the Cowboys their best safety room in years. P.J. Locke adds ST-caliber depth on a 1-year deal. Donovan Wilson's departure is fully absorbed. Safety is no longer a draft-day need at any level — analysts have explicitly removed first-round safety from the board.

Javonte Williams re-signed to a 3yr/$24M extension with $16M guaranteed before the FA market opened. 1,201 rushing yards and 11 TDs in 2025. Two guaranteed years signals a real commitment. RB is completely off the board for the draft.

The Rashan Gary trade (2027 4th-round pick cost) is a meaningful improvement but does not fully solve the need. Gary's reworked 2yr/$32M deal ($16M guaranteed) means he's a real starter, but his second-half disappearing acts in 2025 and overall inconsistency are concerns. Clowney departed unsigned, Donovan Ezeiruaku is out for all OTAs/minicamp with hip surgery, and only James Houston and Sam Williams (1-year, minimal guarantees) remain beyond Gary. Dallas still has a genuine need for a second quality edge rusher — this is a top draft priority at picks 12 or 20, or via continued free agency pursuit.

George Pickens is on the franchise tag at $27.3M for 2026, but the long-term situation is unresolved. Until a multi-year extension is signed (July 15 deadline), a trade remains a non-trivial possibility. CeeDee Lamb is under contract. WR tier stays unchanged pending the Pickens long-term resolution — if he's traded, this escalates to tier 1.

No meaningful LB addition was made in FA. Dallas was reportedly in contact with Quay Walker and Nakobe Dean but both signed with the Raiders. DeMarvion Overshown returns as the lone reliable LB starter. The Cowboys are transitioning to a 3-4 under DC Christian Parker with Marist Liufau converting to OLB/pass rusher. LB remains a priority need for the 2026 draft.

No CB additions were made in free agency. Trevon Diggs was waived during the 2025 season. Josh Butler and Reddy Steward were retained on ERFA tenders (~$1M each) but neither is a proven starter. CB nickel depth was flagged as a need pre-FA and remains fully unaddressed.

Trading Odighizuwa and Thomas reduced IDL depth, but Kenny Clark (restructured) and Quinnen Williams remain as legitimate starters in Parker's 3-4 scheme. Ogbonnia on a 1-year deal provides nose tackle depth. Jay Toia (2025 draft pick) also in the room. IDL tier holds — they still could use a developmental rotational piece in the mid-rounds.

key_additions.headline: Former Pro Bowl edge rusher (46.5 career sacks) fills biggest need on defense via trade for a Day 3 pick
5-year starter with 87 career starts addresses critical safety vacancy alongside Malik Hooker
Cowboys lock up breakout star (1,201 yds, 11 TDs in 2025) before RB market skyrocketed
Pro Bowl receiver (93 rec, 1,429 yds, 9 TDs) retained on tag while long-term deal negotiations continue
Veteran safety and core special teamer reunites with DC Christian Parker to provide depth behind Thompson/Hooker
Former 2nd-round pick retained on prove-it deal as rotational EDGE depth while returning from ACL
Nose tackle addition provides schematic fit as true 3-4 anchor in Parker's new defensive system
Fourth quarterback adds camp competition behind Prescott, Milton, and Grier

key_losses.headline: Cowboys' 2025 sack leader (8.5 sacks, 40 pressures) departs as scheme shifts under DC Christian Parker
5-year starter and 2025 defensive captain traded to 49ers, netting a Day 2 draft pick to rebuild the roster
Starting safety departs in free agency but Cowboys immediately replaced him with Jalen Thompson and P.J. Locke
Veteran depth piece traded to Titans in swap of late-round picks
Disappointing Cowboys tenure ends (3 sacks in 17 games) as team moves on from underperforming edge rusher

notes: Tyrus Wheat re-signing reported but contract terms not yet publicly disclosed — listed as minimum-level deal for now. Clowney remains unsigned as of March 13, 2026. Rashan Gary contract rework details per ESPN/Todd Archer and OTC. George Pickens long-term extension status is the single biggest unresolved roster question that could shift WR need from tier 3 to tier 1 if a trade occurs before the July 15 deadline.
